batch_summary: Full transcription of the Trustee Resignation and Appointment of Successor Trustee (effective 5/1/2017, notarized 10/24/2017) — the instrument previously identified in ISSUE-03 as the missing documentation for the Beard→Willis Trust No. 1 trustee transition. Transcription confirms all previously documented details and advances ISSUE-03 (full text now on file; recording in both counties still required). Three new observations: (1) Beard's recital contains a factual error — it names 'John Marion Black and The Citizens National Bank of Waco' as 1977 co-trustees, when the actual 1977 co-trustees were Olena Bounds Black + Citizens National Bank; JMB had no role in the 1977 trust (he and JAB were co-executors of Olena's 2005 estate); (2) only 3 of 8 named beneficiaries signed the available pages (Beard, JMB, KLR Black); (3) the instrument includes a facsimile/email counterpart execution provision that may explain the missing signatures. No new persons, parcels, or deed entries required.

operations.replace_with: he is tracing the legal lineage of Trust No. 1 back to the founding document of the predecessor trust. ⚠️ **Recital error in Beard's instrument**: The resignation recites the 1977 trust as "executed by...John Marion Black and The Citizens National Bank of Waco, Waco, Texas, as Co-Trustees" — this is incorrect. The actual 1977 co-trustees were **Olena Bounds Black + Citizens National Bank of Waco**. John Marion Black had no trustee role in the 1977 trust; he and James Allen Black served as co-executors of Olena's estate in 2005. The recital error does not affect the instrument's operative clauses (Beard's resignation and Willis's appointment are valid regardless), but should be noted if this instrument is recorded per ISSUE-03.

operations.description: Add transcription observations to ISSUE-03: Beard recital error naming JMB as 1977 co-trustee, signature coverage analysis, and facsimile execution provision
operations.anchor: - **Willis name form in instrument**: "D. Fred Willis" (consistent with LC index "WILLIS DONALD FRED JR" and confirmed legal name "Donald Fredrick Willis Jr.")
operations.content:
- **⚠️ Transcription observations (2026-04-01)**:
    - *Recital error*: Beard's instrument recites the 1977 trust as "executed by John Pierce Black and Olena Bounds Black, as Settlors, and John Marion Black and The Citizens National Bank of Waco, Waco, Texas, as Co-Trustees." This is factually incorrect — the actual 1977 co-trustees were **Olena Bounds Black + Citizens National Bank of Waco**. JMB had no role in the 1977 trust; he and JAB were co-executors of Olena's 2005 estate. The error is in the recital only and does not affect the operative clauses (Beard's resignation and Willis's appointment remain valid).
    - *Signature coverage*: Only 3 of 8 named beneficiaries signed the "Successor Trustee Copy" pages: M. Stephen Beard (resigning trustee), John Marion Black, Kay Lynn Ramsey Black. The remaining beneficiaries (John Ramsey Black, Joel Matthew Black, John Paul Black, Brittany Lachelle Black, Von Ramsey Black, Margaret Kate Black, plus Matthew Holland Black as minor) did NOT sign these pages. Article X compliance depends on whether all beneficiaries must act or a majority suffices — the trust instrument (deeds [06]/[25]) must be reviewed to determine the appointment threshold.
    - *Facsimile/counterpart provision*: Instrument states it "may be executed and exchanged by e-mail or facsimile document transmission and an e-mailed copy of facsimile document signature shall be the equivalent of an original" — suggesting separate counterpart pages with additional signatures may exist.

Only 3 of 8 named beneficiaries signed available pages (Beard, JMB, KLR Black); facsimile/counterpart execution provision present. |
